mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth, measured quarterly, drives corporate earnings expansion and positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon as markets price in persistent economic momentum.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward SPX return is negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDP growth is positive, or the rank IC over any 5-year window is less than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 2.5%, it signals the Fed is tightening policy, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return is positive in more than 60% of instances after the trigger fires, or the average excess return over neutral periods is not negative at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in real GDP and industrial production indicates broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon, with the interaction amplifying the signal beyond individual effects.
candidate_trigger: both series above their 12-month moving average
failure_condition: The interaction term has no incremental predictive power over the individual series (p > 0.05 in a regression), or the directional accuracy is below 55% when both triggers are active.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a CPI inflation reading above 2.5% year-over-year spurs expectations of further rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the tightening cycle is not clearly defined by rising FEDFUNDS, or if 20-day returns during triggered instances are not significantly lower than in non-triggered instances (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payroll employment signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales of more than 1% signals weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue projections and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return after the trigger is not negative, or if the proportion of negative returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payroll employment and housing starts are increasing over a 3-month period, it signals concurrent labor market strength and residential investment, which jointly drive broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: both series show 3-month moving average increase
failure_condition: The joint signal's predictive accuracy is not significantly higher than each series alone (Diebold-Mariano test p > 0.1), or the signal fails to outperform a simple trend-following strategy.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a monthly increase in industrial production above 0.5% confirms that supply constraints are easing and production is rebounding, which boosts equity markets as economic normalisation accelerates, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the regime is not identifiable by a prior sharp decline in INDPRO, or if 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) when the trigger fires within such a regime.
